entry.title: 慢慢滑，比較快：SLUGGISH
entry.content:
介紹

《SLUGGISH》是一本由比利時布魯塞爾滑板團體SLUGS製作的獨立滑板雜誌外，還是他們發行的第一本Skatezine。雜誌主要記錄女孩、女性與酷兒滑板社群的日常生活，內容除了街頭與滑板場的攝影外，還收錄滑手訪談、插畫與拼貼、音樂播放清單、受傷經驗，以及團體舉辦活動和共同建立DIY滑板空間的過程。它並非一本以滑板招式、賽事或職業滑手為主的傳統滑板雜誌，而是從SLUGS自身的社群經驗出發，呈現滑板如何將不同的人聚集在一起，並延伸至藝術創作、音樂、身分認同與社會行動。

“SLUGGISH” is an independent skate magazine created by the Brussels-based skate collective SLUGS, and is their first skate zine. The magazine primarily documents the everyday lives of girls, women, and queer skateboarding communities. Its content includes street and skatepark photography, interviews with skaters, illustrations and collages, music playlists, injury stories, as well as records of events organized by the collective and their collaborative efforts to build DIY skate spaces. Unlike traditional skate magazines that focus on tricks, competitions, or professional skaters, “SLUGGISH” draws from SLUGS’ own community experiences to show how skateboarding brings people together and connects with artistic expression, music, identity, and social activism.

慢慢滑比較快

“Sometimes we like to skate fast, but most of the time we love to take it really, really slow - hence the name of this zine.”

Sluggish 的原意是緩慢、懶散、不急於前進。

他們有時也享受滑行帶來的速度感，但更多時候，更喜歡放慢速度，用自己的步調滑板、相處與創作。對 SLUGS 而言，慢並不代表停滯，而是不急著用招式、速度證明自己。當步調放慢之後，反而更能注意身邊的人、城市裡的細節，以及滑板所帶來的感受。
這也讓我想到一句諺語：慢慢來，反而比較快。《SLUGGISH》想傳達的，是一種不急著抵達目的地的態度，而是透過放慢速度，更仔細地體驗滑板所帶來的一切。

Sluggish suggests moving slowly, without urgency. As SLUGS explain in the opening of the zine:

The name reflects more than a preference for skating at a slower pace. It represents a way of approaching skateboarding without constantly chasing bigger tricks, greater speed, or recognition. By slowing down, SLUGS make space for friendship, creativity, and the small details of everyday life—the conversations between sessions, the overlooked corners of the city, and the feeling of simply spending time together. In this sense, SLUGGISH is not about being stuck, but about moving through skateboarding on their own terms.

SECRET TIP FOR YOUBIKE IN TAIWAN!

滑板文化的核心之一，在於因應不同地形與城市環境，找到屬於自己的移動方式。這種適應不只發生在滑行時，也延伸到滑手如何攜帶滑板、轉乘交通工具，以及在城市中移動。

“When it comes to skateboarding, each one of us have our own way of carrying a board, especially on our bikes, which we use daily.Also, while being on trips, we realised every city has its own special way of helping us out to carry our decks thanks to its infrastructure - subways and trams in different cities have various widths of poles where one can attach its decks to.”

延伸聆聽

雜誌中提到的音樂播放清單可在這邊->🎧收聽。

entry.title: Hello World
entry.content:
Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub.
